MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-18-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANTS WAYNE MCFADDEN AND JONATHAN WEINBERGS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Appearances: Jonathan D. Weinberg of the Law Offices of Wayne A. McFadden (650.340.7100) for cross defendant Wayne McFadden; Thomas Maxwell (595.8935) for cross defendants Wayne McFadden and Jonathan Weinberg; Peter R. Vogel of Bingham McCutchen LLP (650.849.4874) for cross complainant Mary Chiarenza. Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: summary judgment as to Weinberg: Grant, barred by statute of limitations. See UMF 11, 13, 16, 26. Summary Adjudication as to Issues 6, 7, and 9 (whether Cross-Complainant can prove damages): Deny. Cross-defendants fail to meet their statutory burden under CCP 437c(p)(2) of showing that these causes of action have no merit. Burden did not shift to cross-complainant. Summary Adjudication as to Issues 6, 7, and 9 on statute of limitations grounds as to McFadden: Deny. Trindade v. Superior Court, 29 Cal. App. 23d 857, 860 (1973). Summary Adjudication as to Issues 1-5 (whether Cross-Complainant can prove damages): Deny. Cross-defendants fail to meet their statutory burden under CCP 437c(p)(2) of showing that these causes of action have no merit. Burden did not shift to cross-complainant. Summary Adjudication as to Issue 10 (emotional distress damages): Deny. See CCP 437c(f)(1), Catalano v. Superior Court, 82 Cal. App. 4th 91, 96-97 (2000). Summary Adjudication as to Issue 8: Moot, in light of Court's Order of November 10, 2010 (Judge Alvarado) granting judgment on the pleadings as to the 8th cause of action for fraud, with leave to amend. No second amended cross-complaint alleging fraud is before the Court at this time. Summary Judgment as to McFadden: Deny, in light of rulings above. Order signed. The court ruled on cross-defendants' objections to evidence submitted in opposition. Order re: objections filed this date. (302/MJM) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Reporter: Loretta Najera CSR#5153
